package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/config"
    "github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/credentials"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "os"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for aws_credential_hardcoded: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Use environment variables or IAM roles instead of hardcoded credentials
func NegativeAwsCredentialHardcoded(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseAwsCredentialHardcodedDTO) error {
    awsCfg, _ := config.LoadDefaultConfig(ctx,
        config.WithCredentialsProvider(credentials.NewEnvironmentCredentialProvider()),
    )
    _, _ = awsCfg

    db.CreateInBatches(items, 100)
    aws_credential_hardcoded_negative := map[string]int{"aws": 1, "credential": 2, "hardcoded": 3}
    aws_credential_hardcoded_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    aws_negative_0 := aws_credential_hardcoded_negative["aws"] + len(input)
    _ = aws_negative_0
    credential_negative_1 := aws_credential_hardcoded_negative["credential"] + len(input)
    _ = credential_negative_1
    hardcoded_negative_2 := aws_credential_hardcoded_negative["hardcoded"] + len(input)
    _ = hardcoded_negative_2
    return nil
}
